info.title: Arazzo Workflow Control Plane API
info.summary: Inspect and remediate durable Arazzo workflow runs.
info.description:
A REST surface over the Arazzo durability control plane (`ISecuredWorkflowManagement`, plan §11): list and inspect runs, resume a faulted run, cancel a run, and reap terminal ones. It is the workflow-level analogue of dead-letter inspection and redelivery. Every control action takes a single-owner lease and writes under optimistic concurrency, so concurrent operators — or an operator and a worker — cannot conflict. This document is the contract a CLI (`arazzo-runs`) and other clients are generated against; an implementation maps each operation onto the corresponding `ISecuredWorkflowManagement` method over a chosen durability store.

## Authorization responses

Every operation declares a capability-scope `security` requirement (design §14.1) that the deployment's authentication layer enforces uniformly. So in addition to the responses listed on each operation, any secured operation may return **401 Unauthorized** — no credentials were supplied, or the access token is missing, invalid, or expired (the `WWW-Authenticate` header names the scheme) — or **403 Forbidden** — the caller is authenticated but the token lacks the required capability scope. These ambient authentication/authorization responses are uniform across the surface and are not repeated on each operation. Operation-specific **403**s — row-level access (the caller may see a row but is not permitted to act on it, §14.2) — are documented individually where they apply. A deployment that fronts this API with a cookie-session back-end-for-front-end (BFF) additionally requires an anti-forgery header (`X-CSRF`) on cookie-authenticated, state-changing requests; see control-plane design §16.3.

servers.description: The control-plane host (a service the operator deploys alongside its durability store).

tags.name: runs
tags.description: Visibility and remediation for durable workflow runs.

tags.name: catalog
tags.description: Publish and read versioned, runnable workflow definitions: add catalog versions, edit their governance metadata, read packages/workflows/executors/sources, validate inputs, and start runs (capability scopes catalog:read / catalog:write / catalog:purge, plus runs:write to start a run).

tags.name: runners
tags.description: Read the set of registered runners that host and execute catalog workflow versions (capability scope runs:read).

tags.name: security
tags.description: Author the row-security policy: rules and claim→rule bindings (capability scopes security:read / security:write).

tags.name: credentials
tags.description: Manage source credential bindings (design §13): operator-managed references to secret material in an external secret store, plus non-sensitive auth metadata. The control plane stores and returns references only — it never reads, returns, or resolves secret material (capability scopes credentials:read / credentials:write).

tags.name: administrators
tags.description: Manage who may publish and administer a base workflow id (design §15): the set of deployment-stamped administrator identities. Identities are named via the deployment-mapped grant {dimension,value} vocabulary, never raw internal tags; mutations are authorized by current-administrator membership (capability scopes administrators:read / administrators:write).

tags.name: environments
tags.description: Manage governed, reach-scoped deployment environments (design §7.7): create/list/read/update/delete environments and govern each one's administrators (who may manage it and approve promotions into it). Creating an environment grants the creator administration; environments are governed exactly like a workflow (capability scopes environments:read / environments:write, with current-administrator membership applying to governance mutations).

tags.name: sources
tags.description: Manage first-class, reach-scoped source registrations (design §7.6): register/list/read/update/delete the OpenAPI/AsyncAPI documents a workflow references by name in its sourceDescriptions. A source is registered once and resolved immutably by the catalog; its per-environment credentials are managed separately (the credentials surface). Reach-scoped management — there is no administrator set; reach membership is the gate (capability scopes sources:read / sources:write). The registered document is returned only on read of a single source, never in the list.

tags.name: availability
tags.description: Make a workflow version available in a deployment environment — "promotion" (design §7.8). Availability is an additive, many-to-many matrix (workflow version × environment): a version may be available in many environments and an environment may have many versions of one workflow available at once; making one version available never retires another. Making a version available (or withdrawing it) is governed by the TARGET environment's administrators, and is allowed only where the version is ready in that environment — every source the version references has a usable credential there (design §7.7). Reading availability is reach-scoped to the workflow / environment (capability scopes availability:read / availability:write).

tags.name: accessRequests
tags.description: Request and approve elevated, time-bound access to a workflow (design §16.5). Any authenticated principal may submit a request and withdraw their own; an administrator of the target workflow (§15) approves, denies, or revokes. An approval writes a single capped, time-boxed grant (run access only, scoped to the workflow); an eligible requester self-elevates without a human approver. These operations are authorized by the per-workflow administrator gate, not a global capability scope.

tags.name: availabilityRequests
tags.description: Request and approve making a workflow version available in an environment — "promotion" requests (design §7.8). When a principal cannot make a version available directly (they do not administer the target environment), they raise a request; an administrator of that environment approves or denies it, and the requester may withdraw their own. The subject is (workflow version → environment); approval makes the version available (subject to the same §7.7 readiness gate as a direct make). These operations are authorized by the per-environment administrator gate, mirroring the §16.5 access-request inbox, not a global capability scope.

tags.name: runnerAuthorizations
tags.description: Authorize which runners may serve a deployment environment (design §5.5). Receiving an environment's runs means receiving its credentials, so a runner may not self-assert into an environment: it enters Pending on registration and is dispatchable only once an administrator of that environment authorizes it; authorization can be revoked. Listing an environment's runners and authorizing/revoking are governed by that environment's administrators (scopes environments:read / environments:write); the cross-environment approver inbox spans every environment the caller administers, mirroring the §15.4 availability-request inbox.

tags.name: identity
tags.description: Resolve real grantees (person/team/role/workflow) to their exact deployment-stamped identity for the view/operate/administer grant pickers (design §16.5.4), and report the caller's own identity. Read-only: resolution uses a pluggable directory and a store-indexed typeahead of identities the control plane has already observed, so the picker never hand-assembles a {dimension,value} tuple that could silently match no one.

get.description: Aggregates, for a single resolved grantee, all their access: the security bindings whose claim matches the grantee's identity (with per-verb reach), the base workflows the grantee administers, and the source-credential bindings the grantee's runs may use. The grantee is passed as an opaque URL-safe token: the base64url encoding of a resolved grantee's JSON (a ResolvedGrantee object — kind, value, identity, source, complete — exactly as returned by GET /identity/grantees), round-tripped verbatim by the client. Reach-scoped. Binding selection is based on the grantee's resolved sys: identity, so a live token may carry additional non-identity claims (e.g. groups) not represented here, and wildcard ('*') bindings that apply to every principal are omitted.

parameters.name: grantee
parameters.description: An opaque URL-safe token identifying the grantee: base64url of a resolved grantee's JSON (a ResolvedGrantee object from a GET /identity/grantees result), round-tripped verbatim by the client.

get.description: Returns a page of runs matching the visibility query. Backed by the store's wait/visibility index (the same index Tier 2 uses for wakeups), so the filters are answered server-side.

PURGE.description: Reaps completed and cancelled runs last updated strictly before `olderThan`, up to `limit`. Non-terminal runs (including faulted ones, which are terminal-but-recoverable) are never purged.

get.description: Returns a run's management detail — status, cursor, wait/fault detail and etag — from its authoritative checkpoint.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a single run by id, regardless of status. Unlike `PURGE /runs` (which reaps only old terminal runs in bulk), this removes exactly the named run — the operator's explicit "remove this" action. Destructive: requires the `runs:purge` scope.

post.description: Resumes a faulted run, re-executing it from its last checkpoint (the faulted step). Only a run in the `Faulted` state can be resumed. Returns the run's new detail on success (200); a run that does not exist or is out of read reach is 404, a readable run outside write reach is 403, and a run that is not in the `Faulted` state conflicts (409).

200.description: The run was resumed; the body is its new detail.

post.description: Cancels a non-terminal run, marking it `Cancelled` (any fault record is kept for post-mortem visibility). Returns the run's new detail on success.

200.description: The run was cancelled; the body is its new detail.

get.description: Returns a keyset page of the workflow runners currently registered with the control plane (ordered by runnerId): each runner's identity, liveness timestamps, capacity, supported transports, and the catalog versions it hosts. Runners self-register and heartbeat directly against the durability layer; this endpoint reads that registry for observability. Bounded by limit and resumable via the page token.

post.description: Uploads a new immutable workflow document package (the Arazzo workflow plus its referenced OpenAPI/AsyncAPI sources) as `multipart/form-data` — a `package` file part (shaped as the `CatalogPackage` schema) plus `owner` and `tags` parts. The store assigns the next version number, rewrites the workflow id to the versioned form (`{baseWorkflowId}-v{n}`), and records the content hash. The submitted workflow id must not already carry a `-vN` suffix.

package.description: The workflow document package envelope (its shape is the `CatalogPackage` schema), uploaded as a file.

tags.description: Free-form tags for display and filtering.

securityTags.description: Non-internal security tags (§14.2 reach labels) to stamp on the version at add, alongside the deployment's internal tenant tag. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400).

201.description: The version was added; the body is its metadata (the assigned version, versioned id and hash). The documents are fetched via the links.

get.description: Returns a page of catalog version summaries matching the query — free-text over title/description, base workflow id, tags (AND-matched), status, and owner. Keyset paginated.

200.description: A page of matching catalog versions.

PURGE.description: Bulk-deletes obsolete catalog versions that have no referencing workflow runs.

get.summary: List the versions of a workflow

200.description: The versions stored under this base workflow id.

get.description: Returns the version's metadata — content hash, governance metadata, audit fields and the list of documents in the package. The package and its individual documents are fetched via the links.

patch.description: Updates the mutable governance metadata (owner, tags, status). The immutable package, hash, title and description are unchanged. Records `lastUpdatedBy`; setting `status` to `Obsolete` also records `obsoletedBy`.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a single version. Refused with `409` while any workflow run references its versioned workflow id.

get.description: Streams the version's immutable, canonical document package envelope (the Arazzo workflow plus its referenced sources, shaped as the `CatalogPackage` schema) as a single binary download — the self-contained, hash-verifiable artifact that can be handed to the code-generation service.

get.summary: Get a version's Arazzo workflow document
get.description: Returns just the Arazzo workflow document for this version (with the versioned workflow id), without the OpenAPI/AsyncAPI sources. Convenient for the UI when only the workflow definition is needed.

get.summary: Get a version's precomputed schema metadata
get.description: Returns the precomputed schema-metadata document baked into the package — the typed shape of each workflow's inputs and each step's resolved outputs (and, progressively, requests and responses) — so UIs can render strongly-typed forms (a typed patch/output builder, a workflow editor) without re-parsing the OpenAPI/AsyncAPI sources. 404 if the version carries no baked metadata.

get.summary: Download a version's compiled workflow executor assembly
get.description: Streams the compiled workflow executor assembly (a .NET DLL) baked into the package as a single binary download — the artifact an execution host dynamically loads to run the workflow. 404 if the version is not runnable (carries no baked executor).

Returns the executor manifest baked alongside the compiled assembly — the target framework, the assembly digest binding the DLL to this version, the entry type, and the declared sources — so a runner can verify and load the executor. 404 if the version is not runnable (carries no baked executor).

post.summary: Validate a value against a schema in a version
post.description: Validates a JSON value against the true JSON Schema of a target within this version's package — a workflow's `inputs`, a step's request or response body, or a step's `outputs` object — using a full JSON Schema validator (not the lossy precomputed metadata). Returns a structured result (valid plus any errors with their instance locations); a malformed value still yields `200` with `valid: false`. Returns `404` when the version or the requested target schema cannot be resolved.

post.summary: Start a run of a workflow version
post.description: Triggers a new run of this runnable version in a deployment environment (design §5.5): validates the supplied inputs against the version's baked inputs schema, pins the run to the required `environment`, then creates a Pending run that a runner serving that environment claims and executes asynchronously and durably. The run is pinned to the environment at start — it selects the credential set and constrains dispatch to runners authorized to serve it. Returns 202 with the run id; observe the run via the runs endpoints. 404 if the version does not exist, or the environment does not exist or is outside the caller's reach; 409 if the version is not available in the environment (§7.8), is not runnable (carries no executor), or no registered runner currently serves the environment; 422 if the inputs fail validation.

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: The deployment environment to pin the run to (design §5.5). The version must be available in this environment (§7.8) and the environment must be within the caller's reach; the run uses this environment's credential set and is dispatched only to runners serving it.

202.description: The run was accepted and is pending execution on a hosting runner.

422.description: The inputs failed validation against the version's baked inputs schema.

get.summary: Get a single source document from a version
get.description: Returns one named source document (an OpenAPI or AsyncAPI document) referenced by this version's workflow. The available source names are listed in the version metadata's `sources` array.

get.summary: List the environments a workflow version is available in
get.description: Returns a page of availability entries for this version — the deployment environments it has been made available in (design §7.8), ordered by environment. Visible to a caller who can read the workflow version.

put.summary: Make a workflow version available in an environment
put.description: Makes this workflow version available in the environment (design §7.8) — additive, never retiring another version. The caller must be a current administrator of the TARGET environment (403 otherwise), and the version must be ready in that environment: every source it references must resolve a usable credential there (design §7.7), else 409 with the missing sources. Idempotent — making an already-available version available again returns the existing entry (200).

200.description: The version was already available (the existing entry).

delete.summary: Withdraw a workflow version's availability in an environment
delete.description: Removes this version's availability in the environment (design §7.8). The caller must be a current administrator of the target environment (403 otherwise). Does not affect any other version or environment.

get.summary: List the configured ordered tag dimensions
get.description: Returns the deployment's ordered tag dimensions (e.g. classification) and their labels in ascending order, so an authoring UI can offer ordered (<, <=, >, >=) rule templates with the exact labels the policy enforces.
Returns a keyset page of rules ordered by name, bounded by limit and resumable via the page token; q filters by a case-insensitive substring of the name or expression.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text filter matched against each rule's name and expression (case-insensitive contains).

post.description: Creates a named rule; the expression is validated against the security-rule grammar.

put.description: Replaces the rule's content; the expression is validated against the security-rule grammar.

get.description: Returns a keyset page of claim→rule bindings ordered by (order, id), bounded by limit and resumable via the page token; q filters by a case-insensitive substring of the claim type, claim value, or description.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text filter matched against each binding's claim type, claim value, and description (case-insensitive contains).

post.description: Creates a claim→rule binding; the store assigns its id.

get.description: Returns a page of source credential bindings (references and non-secret metadata only), ordered by sourceName then environment. Page with `limit` and the opaque `pageToken` from a previous page's `nextPageToken`.

post.description: Creates a binding for (sourceName, environment). The body carries references (a SecretRef of the form scheme://locator[#version]) and non-secret metadata only; a value that is not a well-formed SecretRef is rejected (400). Conflicts if a binding for that (sourceName, environment) already exists.

put.description: Replaces the references and non-secret metadata of an existing binding; the (sourceName, environment) identity and the created-* audit fields are immutable. Rotation is by changing the reference. A value that is not a well-formed SecretRef is rejected (400).

get.description: Returns a page of deployment environments the caller's reach admits (§14.2), ordered by name. Page with `limit` and the opaque `pageToken` from a previous page's `nextPageToken`.

post.description: Creates a governed, reach-scoped environment and grants the calling principal administration of it (design §7.7). The deployment stamps the creator's internal tenant tag onto managementTags and rejects a set the creator's own reach does not admit (no privilege escalation). Conflicts if an environment with that name already exists in the caller's reach.

put.description: Replaces the mutable metadata (display name, description) of an existing environment; the name, managementTags reach scope, and created-* audit fields are immutable. The caller must be a current administrator of the environment (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

delete.description: Deletes an environment. The caller must be a current administrator of the environment (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

get.description: Returns the administrator identities entitled to govern the environment (design §7.7) — who may manage it and approve promotions into it. Each identity is described as the deployment-mapped grant {dimension,value} it resolves from, never raw internal tags.

put.summary: Transfer environment administration (replace the administrator set)
put.description: Replaces the entire administrator set for the environment with the given identities (at least one) — an administrator may transfer administration away from itself (design §7.7). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

post.description: Adds an identity to the environment's administrator set (idempotent). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

200.description: The administrator set after the add.

delete.description: Removes the administrator identified by its identity digest (from AdministratorGrant.digest in the list) from the environment's administrator set. The set may not be left empty — removing the last administrator conflicts (409). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise). An unknown digest is a no-op (the resulting set is returned).

200.description: The administrator set after the removal.

get.summary: List the workflow versions available in an environment
get.description: Returns a page of availability entries for this environment — the (workflow, version) pairs made available in it (design §7.8), ordered by base workflow id then version. Visible to a caller whose reach admits the environment.

get.summary: List the runners that serve an environment and their authorization status
get.description: Returns a page of the runner authorizations for this environment (design §5.5) — each runner that has registered for it with its Pending/Authorized/Revoked status, ordered by runnerId. The caller must be a current administrator of the environment (403 otherwise; 404 if it is not in the caller's reach). Optionally filtered by status.

post.summary: Authorize a runner to serve an environment
post.description: Authorizes the runner to serve this environment (design §5.5) — it becomes dispatchable for runs targeting the environment. The caller must be a current administrator of the environment (403 otherwise; 404 if it is not in the caller's reach, or if no runner with that id has registered for it). Idempotent — authorizing an already-Authorized runner returns the existing record.

delete.summary: Revoke a runner's authorization to serve an environment
delete.description: Revokes the runner's authorization for this environment (design §5.5), immediately removing it from dispatch; the record persists with status Revoked. The caller must be a current administrator of the environment (403 otherwise; 404 if it is not in the caller's reach, or no such runner authorization exists). Idempotent.

get.description: Returns a page of registered sources the caller's reach admits (§14.2), ordered by name. Each entry is a SourceSummary — the registered document is NOT included; fetch a single source to read it. Page with `limit` and the opaque `pageToken` from a previous page's `nextPageToken`.

post.description: Registers a reach-scoped source (design §7.6): its name, type, and OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document, plus the managementTags scoping who may see and manage it. The deployment stamps the creator's internal tenant tag onto managementTags and rejects a set the creator's own reach does not admit (no privilege escalation). Conflicts if a source with that name already exists in the caller's reach.

get.description: Returns the full source, including its registered OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document, if it is in the caller's reach.

put.description: Replaces the registered document and mutable metadata (display name, description) of an existing source; the name, type, managementTags reach scope, and created-* audit fields are immutable. The source must be in the caller's reach (404 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

delete.description: Deletes a source registration. The source must be in the caller's reach (404 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409). Per-environment credentials bound to the source are managed separately and are not removed here.

get.description: Returns the administrator identities entitled to publish further versions of the base workflow id and to manage its administration (design §15). Each identity is described as the deployment-mapped grant {dimension,value} it resolves from — never raw internal tags.

put.description: Replaces the entire administrator set for the base workflow id with the given identities (at least one) — an administrator may transfer administration away from itself (design §15). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

post.description: Adds an identity to the base workflow id's administrator set (idempotent). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise); a stale concurrent change conflicts (409).

200.description: The administrator set after the add.

delete.description: Removes the administrator identified by its identity digest (from AdministratorGrant.digest in the list) from the base workflow id's administrator set. Removing a multi-tag grantee removes the whole identity in one call. The set may not be left empty — removing the last administrator conflicts (409). The caller must be a current administrator (403 otherwise). An unknown digest is a no-op (the resulting set is returned).

200.description: The administrator set after the removal.

post.description: Submits a request for elevated, time-bound access to a workflow (design §16.5). Any authenticated principal may submit; the requesting subject is taken from the caller, so a grant can never target a third party. If the requester is eligible to self-elevate exactly this, the request is auto-approved (self-elevation); otherwise it is created pending an administrator's decision.

201.description: The created request (pending, or already approved when self-elevated).

get.description: Lists access requests visible to the caller, oldest first. With baseWorkflowId, returns that workflow's request queue — the caller must be an administrator of it (403 otherwise). Without baseWorkflowId, scope selects the view: 'mine' (default) the caller's own requests; 'queue' the approver inbox — every request across the workflows the caller administers. Optionally filtered by status.

parameters.name: baseWorkflowId
parameters.description: The target workflow whose request queue to list (requires administrator of it); absent lists per scope.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: When baseWorkflowId is absent, which requests to list: 'mine' (default) the caller's own; 'queue' the approver inbox across every workflow the caller administers. Ignored when baseWorkflowId is present.

get.description: Returns a single access request. The caller must be its requester or an administrator of its target workflow (403 otherwise).

post.description: Approves a pending request, writing the capped, time-boxed grant (run access only, scoped to the workflow). The caller must be an administrator of the target workflow (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409); a request whose scopes are not grantable is rejected (400).

post.summary: Approve an access request as durable eligibility
post.description: Approves a pending request as durable eligibility (design §16.5.3) rather than as a live grant: writes a capped eligibility assignment scoped to the workflow, after which the requester may self-elevate JIT without re-approval (each activation independently time-boxed). The caller must be an administrator of the target workflow (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409); a request whose scopes are not grantable is rejected (400).
Denies a pending request. The caller must be an administrator of the target workflow (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409).
Withdraws a pending request. Only the requester (the request's own subject) may withdraw it (403 otherwise); a request that is not pending conflicts (409).

Revokes an approved grant early: the entitlement is deleted (access stops at the next resolution, fail-safe) and the request is marked revoked. The caller must be an administrator of the target workflow (403 otherwise). A request that is not an approved grant conflicts (409).

Requests that a workflow version be made available in an environment (design §7.8), for a principal who cannot make it available directly because they do not administer the target environment. The requesting identity is taken from the caller. The request is created pending an environment administrator's decision; approval makes the version available (subject to the §7.7 readiness gate at approval time).

201.description: The created request (pending an environment administrator's decision).

get.description: Lists availability requests visible to the caller, oldest first. With environment, returns that environment's request queue — the caller must be an administrator of it (403 otherwise). Without environment, scope selects the view: 'mine' (default) the caller's own requests; 'queue' the approver inbox — every request across the environments the caller administers. Optionally filtered by status.

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: The target environment whose request queue to list (requires administrator of it); absent lists per scope.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: When environment is absent, which requests to list: 'mine' (default) the caller's own; 'queue' the approver inbox across every environment the caller administers. Ignored when environment is present.

get.description: Returns a single availability request. The caller must be its requester or an administrator of its target environment (403 otherwise).

post.description: Approves a pending request, making the workflow version available in the target environment. The caller must be an administrator of that environment (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409); a request whose version is not ready in the environment — a referenced source has no usable credential there (§7.7) — is rejected (409).
Denies a pending request. The caller must be an administrator of the target environment (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409).
Withdraws the caller's own pending request. The caller must be its requester (403 otherwise). A request that is not pending conflicts (409).

get.description: Lists runner authorizations across the environments the caller administers — the approver inbox for authorizing runners (design §5.5/§15.4), ordered by (environment, runnerId). With environment, returns that environment's authorizations and the caller must administer it (403 otherwise). Without environment, returns the inbox spanning every environment the caller administers; defaults to Pending so an administrator sees the runners awaiting authorization. Optionally filtered by status.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Only authorizations in this state (the inbox defaults to Pending when omitted).

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: A single environment whose authorizations to list (requires administrator of it); absent lists the inbox across every administered environment.

get.description: Returns the caller's own deployment-stamped identity as {dimension,value} grants (design §16.5.4) — the identity an administrator entry or grant must name to match the caller (so a UI can offer 'add me' and warn before a transfer would lock the caller out). Empty when the deployment is unscoped.

get.summary: The grantee kinds the deployment can resolve
get.description: Returns the grantee kinds (person/team/role/workflow) the deployment can resolve to a sys: identity, and whether an external directory is configured (design §16.5.4) — so the grantee picker offers exactly the resolvable grain instead of guessing.

Searches for grantees matching a prefix, each resolved to its exact sys: identity (design §16.5.4). Source `observed` (default) is the store-indexed typeahead of identities the control plane has already seen (access requesters, version authors, administrators, grantees); `directory` queries the external directory when one is configured. Page with `limit` and the opaque `pageToken`.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The case-sensitive value prefix to match (empty matches all).

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Where to resolve from: the store-indexed observed-identity typeahead (default) or the external directory.

oauth2.description: OAuth2 bearer access tokens scoped to the control plane's capability tiers. Interactive operators use the authorization-code flow (with PKCE, or device-code at the IdP); services and the CLI use client-credentials. The authorization server is chosen by the deployment — the URLs below are placeholders.

scopes.catalog:read: Search and read catalog workflow versions.
scopes.catalog:write: Add catalog versions and edit their governance metadata.
scopes.credentials:read: List and read source credential bindings (references and metadata only — never secret material).
scopes.credentials:write: Create, update, and delete source credential bindings (references and metadata only — never secret material).
scopes.security:read: List and read the row-security policy: rules and claim→rule bindings.
scopes.security:write: Create, update, and delete row-security rules and bindings.
scopes.administrators:read: Read a workflow's administrator set (described as deployment-mapped grants — never raw internal tags).
scopes.administrators:write: Manage a workflow's administrator set: add, remove, and transfer administration (current-administrator membership applies).
scopes.environments:read: List and read governed deployment environments (reach-scoped).
scopes.environments:write: Create, update, and delete deployment environments and manage their administrators (current-administrator membership applies).
scopes.sources:read: List and read registered sources (reach-scoped; the source document is returned only on single read).
scopes.availability:read: List where workflow versions are available across environments (reach-scoped).
scopes.availability:write: Make a workflow version available in an environment, or withdraw it (target-environment-administrator gated; readiness-gated).

scopes.catalog:read: Search and read catalog workflow versions.
scopes.catalog:write: Add catalog versions and edit their governance metadata.
scopes.credentials:read: List and read source credential bindings (references and metadata only — never secret material).
scopes.credentials:write: Create, update, and delete source credential bindings (references and metadata only — never secret material).
scopes.security:read: List and read the row-security policy: rules and claim→rule bindings.
scopes.security:write: Create, update, and delete row-security rules and bindings.
scopes.administrators:read: Read a workflow's administrator set (described as deployment-mapped grants — never raw internal tags).
scopes.administrators:write: Manage a workflow's administrator set: add, remove, and transfer administration (current-administrator membership applies).
scopes.environments:read: List and read governed deployment environments (reach-scoped).
scopes.environments:write: Create, update, and delete deployment environments and manage their administrators (current-administrator membership applies).
scopes.sources:read: List and read registered sources (reach-scoped; the source document is returned only on single read).
scopes.availability:read: List where workflow versions are available across environments (reach-scoped).
scopes.availability:write: Make a workflow version available in an environment, or withdraw it (target-environment-administrator gated; readiness-gated).

openIdConnect.description: OpenID Connect discovery for the same access tokens and scopes (runs:read / runs:write / runs:purge); the discovery URL is deployment-specific (placeholder shown).

mtls.description: Mutual-TLS client-certificate authentication, for zero-trust service/operator callers that prefer certificates over tokens. Mutual TLS carries no scopes, so the host maps the client certificate's identity to a capability tier (read/write/purge) out of band.

CredentialSourceNamePath.name: sourceName
CredentialSourceNamePath.description: The Arazzo source description name the credential authenticates calls to.

EnvironmentPath.name: environment
EnvironmentPath.description: The deployment environment the binding applies to.

StatusQuery.name: status
StatusQuery.description: Restrict to runs in this lifecycle status.

WorkflowIdQuery.name: workflowId
WorkflowIdQuery.description: Restrict to runs of this workflow.

LimitQuery.name: limit
LimitQuery.description: The maximum number of entries to return in this page (also the maximum to delete, for purge).

PageTokenQuery.name: pageToken
PageTokenQuery.description: An opaque continuation token from a previous page's `nextPageToken`, for paging.

CreatedAfterQuery.name: createdAfter
CreatedAfterQuery.description: Restrict to runs created at or after this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

CreatedBeforeQuery.name: createdBefore
CreatedBeforeQuery.description: Restrict to runs created strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

UpdatedAfterQuery.name: updatedAfter
UpdatedAfterQuery.description: Restrict to runs last updated at or after this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

UpdatedBeforeQuery.name: updatedBefore
UpdatedBeforeQuery.description: Restrict to runs last updated strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

TagQuery.name: tag
TagQuery.description: Restrict to runs carrying every supplied tag (repeat the parameter to require multiple, e.g. `?tag=tenant-42&tag=priority`).

CorrelationIdQuery.name: correlationId
CorrelationIdQuery.description: Restrict to runs with this telemetry correlation id (exact match), to pivot from a trace to its run.

OlderThanQuery.name: olderThan
OlderThanQuery.description: Reap terminal runs last updated strictly before this instant (RFC 3339 date-time).

BaseWorkflowIdPath.name: baseWorkflowId
BaseWorkflowIdPath.description: The base workflow id (without any version suffix).

AdministratorDigestPath.name: digest
AdministratorDigestPath.description: The stable identity digest of an administrator (from AdministratorGrant.digest) — the key for removing it.

SourceNamePath.name: name
SourceNamePath.description: The source's registered name (matches a workflow's sourceDescriptions entry).

VersionNumberPath.name: versionNumber
VersionNumberPath.description: The 1-based version number under the base workflow id.

CatalogSourceNamePath.name: sourceName
CatalogSourceNamePath.description: The name of a source document within the version's package (as listed in the version metadata's `sources` array).

CatalogSearchQuery.name: q
CatalogSearchQuery.description: Free-text filter matched against each version's title and description (case-insensitive contains).

CatalogBaseWorkflowIdQuery.name: baseWorkflowId
CatalogBaseWorkflowIdQuery.description: Restrict to versions of this base workflow id.

CatalogWorkflowIdPrefixQuery.name: workflowIdPrefix
CatalogWorkflowIdPrefixQuery.description: Restrict to versions whose versioned workflow id starts with this prefix (case-insensitive). Designed for type-ahead: it is an anchored prefix match (index-friendly) over the versioned workflow id, which begins with the base workflow id — so typing a base name matches all of its versions.

CatalogTagQuery.name: tag
CatalogTagQuery.description: Restrict to versions carrying every supplied tag (repeat to require multiple).

CatalogStatusQuery.name: status
CatalogStatusQuery.description: Restrict to versions in this lifecycle status.

CatalogOwnerQuery.name: owner
CatalogOwnerQuery.description: Restrict to versions whose owner name or email matches (case-insensitive contains).

CatalogDistinctWorkflowsQuery.name: distinctWorkflows
CatalogDistinctWorkflowsQuery.description: Collapse the result to one row per base workflow instead of one per version. When true, each page carries a single representative version per base workflow — the newest Active version, else the newest Obsolete, else the newest — and the page is keyset-ordered by base workflow id (the continuation cursor is the base id alone). A base workflow appears if any of its versions match the other filters, and its representative is the best-matching version. When omitted or false, one row per version is returned, keyset by base workflow id then version.

Forbidden.description: The caller may see the row but is not permitted to perform this action on it (row-level write authorization).

value.title: Action not permitted
value.detail: You do not have permission to modify run 'adopt-42'.

NotFound.description: No run with that id exists.

Conflict.description: The action does not apply in the run's current state, or the run is held by another owner (lease) or was changed concurrently.

notFaulted.summary: Resume requested for a run that is not faulted

value.title: Run is not resumable
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is Completed; only a Faulted run can be resumed.

leaseHeld.summary: Another owner holds the run's lease

value.title: Run is busy
value.detail: Run 'adopt-42' is currently held by another owner.

NextCatalogPage.description: Fetch the next page of catalog versions; absent in effect when nextPageToken is null.

CatalogPackage.description: Download this version's full document package as a binary stream.

CatalogWorkflow.description: Fetch just this version's Arazzo workflow document.

NextPage.description: Fetch the next page of runs; absent in effect when nextPageToken is null (the last page).

ResumeRun.description: Resume this run from its last checkpoint (only applies if it is faulted).

AccessGrantsOverview.description: The who-can-do-what aggregation for one grantee (design §6.1): the security bindings that grant it reach, the workflows it administers, and the credentials its runs may use.

bindings.description: The security bindings whose claim matches the grantee's identity; each carries its per-verb read/write/purge reach. Delete a binding to revoke that access.

administers.description: The base workflows the grantee administers (exact identity set-equality).

credentialUsage.description: The source-credential bindings the grantee's runs may use (label-superset over the binding's usage grant).

AccessGrantsAdministeredWorkflow.description: A base workflow a grantee administers (a link target for the catalog).

AccessGrantsCredentialUsage.description: A source-credential binding a grantee's runs may use, keyed by (source name, environment).

environment.description: The environment the binding is scoped to.

WorkflowRunStatus.description: A run's lifecycle status. `Faulted` is terminal-but-recoverable (awaiting remediation); `Completed` and `Cancelled` are terminal.

WorkflowWaitKind.description: What a suspended run is waiting for.

dueAt.description: When a timer wait becomes due (set for `Timer`).

channel.description: The channel a message wait listens on (set for `Message`).

correlationId.description: The correlation id a message wait matches, if any.

attempt.description: The attempt count at the fault.

WorkflowRunSummary.description: A run as projected into the visibility index (one entry in a page).

environment.description: The deployment environment the run is pinned to (design §5.5): its credential set, and the runners it can be dispatched to.

errorType.description: The fault error, if the run is/was faulted.

correlationId.description: The run-wide telemetry correlation id set at creation (the value stamped on the run's `corvus.arazzo.correlation_id` spans), if any.

tags.description: The free-form tags applied to the run at creation.

WorkflowRunPage.description: A page of runs matching a visibility query.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

WorkflowRunDetail.description: A run's management detail, read from its authoritative checkpoint.

environment.description: The deployment environment the run is pinned to (design §5.5): its credential set, and the runners it can be dispatched to.

cursor.description: The state-machine index of the next step to run.

wait.description: Why the run is suspended, if it is.

fault.description: The fault record if the run is (or was) faulted.

etag.description: The optimistic-concurrency token the checkpoint was read at.

correlationId.description: The run-wide telemetry correlation id set at creation (the value stamped on the run's `corvus.arazzo.correlation_id` spans), if any.

tags.description: The free-form tags applied to the run at creation.

ResumeRequest.description: How to resume a faulted run — a union on `mode`. Each form carries only the fields its mode needs; the `const` mode discriminates the variant.

RetryFaultedStepResume.description: Re-execute from the last checkpoint — the faulted step. The common case.

RewindResume.description: Reset the cursor to an earlier step and re-run forward from there.

SkipResume.description: Advance the cursor past the faulted step, optionally recording operator-supplied outputs for it.

targetCursor.description: The cursor to resume at (defaults to the faulted cursor + 1).

skipOutputs.description: The operator-supplied outputs to record for the skipped step so downstream references resolve. Any JSON value.

StatePatchResume.description: Apply an RFC 6902 JSON Patch to the run's context object { "inputs": …, "stepOutputs": { … } }, then retry the faulted step.

CancelRequest.description: A request to cancel a run.

reason.description: An operator-supplied reason for the cancellation (recorded for audit). Must be non-empty.

purgedCount.description: The number of runs or versions deleted.

ValidationTarget.description: Identifies the schema within a version's package to validate a value against.

kind.description: Which schema: a workflow's inputs, a step's request body, a step's response body, or a step's outputs object.

workflowId.description: The (versioned) workflow id; defaults to the version's workflow when omitted.

stepId.description: The step id. Required for requestBody, responseBody and stepOutputs.

status.description: The response status code for a responseBody target; defaults to the success (lowest 2xx, else default) response.

ValidationRequest.description: A request to validate a JSON value against a target schema in a version's package.

value.description: The JSON value to validate (any JSON type).

instancePath.description: A JSON Pointer to the failing location within the posted value.

message.description: A human-readable description of the failure.

keyword.description: The JSON Schema keyword that failed, when known.

schemaLocation.description: The evaluation location within the schema that produced the failure, when known.

ValidationResult.description: The outcome of validating a value against a schema.

valid.description: Whether the value conformed to the schema.

WorkflowRunAccepted.description: Acknowledges a triggered run that will execute asynchronously and durably on a hosting runner.

runId.description: The id of the created run; poll the runs endpoints to observe its progress.

workflowId.description: The versioned workflow id the run executes.

PageLimit.description: A page size: the maximum number of items to return (or delete, for purge).

TagList.description: A set of free-form tags (AND-matched when used as a filter).

CatalogOwner.description: The accountable governance owner of a workflow, for integration with governance tooling.

name.description: The owning person or team's display name.

url.description: An optional link (e.g. a runbook or owning-team page).

CatalogPackage.description: The logical content of a workflow document package: the Arazzo workflow plus its referenced source documents. The package is transferred and stored as an opaque ZIP archive (manifest.json + workflow.json + sources/<name>.json); this schema documents that archive's logical content. Only the catalog-relevant fields of the contained documents are typed; additional properties are permitted, so any Arazzo/OpenAPI/AsyncAPI version validates and the package stays a version-agnostic input to code generation.

sources.description: The referenced source documents (OpenAPI/AsyncAPI), keyed by the workflow's sourceDescriptions name.

CatalogPackageWorkflow.description: An Arazzo workflow document. The catalog-relevant fields are typed; additional properties are permitted.

info.description: The document info; the catalog projects title and description (falling back to summary).

sourceDescriptions.description: The source documents the workflow references.

workflows.description: The workflows in the document; the catalog uses the first workflow's id as the base id.

CatalogPackageSourceDescription.description: A named source description; the catalog surfaces the name and type.

CatalogPackageWorkflowEntry.description: A workflow within the Arazzo document; the catalog reads its id.

CatalogSourceRef.description: A reference to one source document within a version's package, as projected into the metadata.

name.description: The source name (the workflow's sourceDescriptions name), used to address the document.

type.description: The source document kind (e.g. `openapi`, `asyncapi`), if declared by the workflow.

CatalogSecurityTag.description: A non-internal security label (KVP) on a catalog version for row authorization (§14.2) — a reach label rules match on, e.g. domain=payments; a run inherits its version's labels. Distinct from free-form display `tags`, and from environment/source/credential `managementTags` (which scope who may MANAGE a row). Internal/reserved-prefix tags (e.g. tenant) are deployment-owned and never set here — see the operation descriptions.

CatalogMetadataPatch.description: A partial update of a version's mutable governance metadata; omitted fields are left unchanged. Closed (additionalProperties: false) so an unknown field is rejected with 400 rather than silently ignored. `securityTags` sets the version's NON-internal reach labels (§14.2) — a governed edit for a workflow administrator; the reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400), since internal tags (e.g. the deployment tenant) stay deployment-stamped and immutable.

securityTags.description: Replace the version's non-internal security tags (§14.2 reach labels). The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); internal tags remain deployment-owned.

CatalogVersionSummary.description: A catalog version as projected into the searchable index (one entry in a page).

workflowId.description: The versioned workflow id runs execute under.

securityTags.description: The version's non-internal security tags (§14.2) — the reach labels row-security rules match on. Internal/reserved-prefix tags are deployment-owned and stripped from this view. A run inherits its version's security tags.

sources.description: The source documents in this version's package, addressable individually via the source endpoint.

runnable.description: Whether this version carries a compiled workflow executor assembly (downloadable via the executor endpoint) that an execution host can run.

hash.description: The SHA-256 of the canonical package, hex-encoded.

CatalogPage.description: A page of catalog versions matching a search.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page; absent on the last page.

RunnerHostedVersion.description: A catalog version a runner currently hosts.

hash.description: The content hash of the hosted version's package.

loaded.description: Whether the runner has the version loaded and ready to execute.

RunnerSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a runner for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

Runner.description: A workflow runner process registered with the control plane.

runnerId.description: The stable identifier of the runner process.

environment.description: The single deployment environment this runner serves (design §5.5); the runner is dispatchable only for runs targeting it and inherits its reach.

reachTags.description: The runner's row-security reach (§14.2), stamped from the serving environment's managementTags at registration; a tenant sees only runners serving its environments. Absent on a runner serving an unscoped environment.

address.description: An optional address (for example a base URI) at which the runner can be reached.

lastSeenAt.description: The instant of the runner's most recent heartbeat.

maxConcurrency.description: The maximum number of runs the runner will execute concurrently.

transports.description: The transport identifiers the runner supports.

hostedVersions.description: The catalog versions the runner currently hosts.

RunnerPage.description: A keyset page of the workflow runners currently registered with the control plane, ordered by runnerId.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

EnvironmentRunnerAuthorizationView.description: A runner's authorization to serve a deployment environment (design §5.5): keyed by (environment, runnerId), governed by the environment's administrators. A runner enters Pending on registration and is dispatchable only once Authorized; Revoked removes it from dispatch.

runnerId.description: The runner the authorization applies to.

status.description: The authorization's lifecycle state; only Authorized is dispatchable.

reason.description: An optional note recorded with the most recent decision.

createdBy.description: The actor that created the record (the runner self-registering enters Pending).

createdAt.description: When the record was created (the runner first registered for the environment).

decidedBy.description: The environment administrator that last authorized or revoked the runner, if decided.

decidedAt.description: When the runner was last authorized or revoked, if decided.

EnvironmentRunnerAuthorizationList.description: A keyset page of runner authorizations, ordered by runnerId (for an environment) or by (environment, runnerId) (for the approver inbox).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

RunnerAuthorizationDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an authorize or revoke decision.

reason.description: An optional human-readable justification for the decision.

VerbGrant.description: A per-verb grant in a binding: either unrestricted (full reach — the operator escape) or the conjunction of the named rules. The default (unrestricted false, empty ruleNames) grants nothing for the verb.

unrestricted.description: When true the verb is unrestricted (full reach); ruleNames is ignored.

ruleNames.description: The rule names ANDed for the verb.

expression.description: The rule text in the security-rule grammar.

SecurityRuleUpdate.description: Replacement content for an existing security rule.

expression.description: The rule text in the security-rule grammar.

SecurityRuleList.description: A keyset page of security rules, ordered by name.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

SecurityOrdering.description: An ordered tag dimension: one dimension's labels in ascending order, the ranking an ordered rule comparison (<, <=, >, >=) uses.

labels.description: The dimension's labels from lowest to highest rank.

SecurityOrderingList.description: The deployment's configured ordered tag dimensions (design §14.2).

SecurityBindingWrite.description: The content of a claim→rule binding (create or replace).

claimType.description: The principal claim type this binding keys on ('*' matches any authenticated principal).

claimValue.description: The required claim value; omit to match any value of claimType.

SecurityBindingList.description: A keyset page of claim→rule bindings, ordered by (order, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

SourceCredentialKind.description: The HTTP auth scheme the resolved secret(s) build into a provider (non-sensitive metadata). 'mtls' is connection-level (a client certificate presented at the TLS handshake, §13.1): it carries a 'certificate' secret (a base64 PKCS#12, or a PEM 'certificate' paired with a PEM 'privateKey') plus an optional 'passphrase', and — being connection-scoped — cannot be usage-scoped to an individual run.

CredentialStatus.description: The derived health of a binding's referenced secret (§13.2), computed from expiresAt against the current time — never persisted. 'valid' = not near expiry (or no known expiry); 'expiringSoon' = within the rotation-warning window; 'expired' = past expiry.

SecretReference.description: A named, role-tagged reference to one secret in the external store. The ref is a pointer (scheme://locator[#version]) — never secret material.

name.description: The role this secret plays (e.g. value, password, clientSecret).

ref.description: The secret reference: scheme://locator[#version] (keyvault, awssm, vault, env, file).

CredentialConfigEntry.description: A single non-secret auth configuration value.

key.description: The configuration key (e.g. headerName, tokenUrl, scopes, username).

CredentialSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a binding for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme, team=ops.

CredentialUsageGrant.description: An identity grant of which runs may USE a binding (§13). The deployment maps it to an unforgeable internal tag a run carries (e.g. {workflow, nightly-reconcile} → sys:workflow=nightly-reconcile) — usage cannot be self-granted by free-form tags.

value.description: The identity value (e.g. the base workflow id, or a tenant id).

CredentialUsageGrantee.description: The resolved grantee whose runs may USE a binding (§13/§16.5.4): its identity as the deployment-mapped {dimension,value} grants the deployment resolves to unforgeable internal tags, plus the resolved kind/label for display. Mirrors AdministratorGrant. A run may use the binding only if it carries every tag of this identity (label-superset). Omit on a binding to default to the creating principal's identity (the owner's runs).

identity.description: The grantee's resolved identity as {dimension,value} grants (a multi-tag grantee — e.g. a person — yields several, AND-matched).

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label for the resolved grantee.

CredentialBindingCreate.description: A source credential binding to create — references and non-secret metadata only. Secret material must never appear here; each secretRefs entry is a SecretRef pointer the runner dereferences.

secretRefs.description: The named references to secret material in the external store (at least one).

config.description: Non-secret auth configuration. Secret material must never appear here.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2), independent of the usage grant (usageGrantee). The deployment additionally stamps the creating principal's internal tenant tag, and rejects a set the creator's own reach does not admit (no privilege escalation). The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

expiresAt.description: When the referenced long-lived secret expires, if knowable (e.g. a cert NotAfter, an API-key/refresh-token lifetime). Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata (§13.2) driving the derived credentialStatus; omit when expiry is unknown (treated as non-expiring).

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata for operator visibility; distinct from lastUpdatedAt.

CredentialBindingUpdate.description: Replacement references and non-secret metadata for an existing binding (its sourceName/environment identity is taken from the path and is immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2); absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

expiresAt.description: When the (rotated) referenced secret expires, if knowable. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata (§13.2); omit when unknown.

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known. Non-sensitive lifecycle metadata for operator visibility.

CredentialBindingSummary.description: A persisted source credential binding — references and non-secret metadata only. No secret material is ever included.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE this binding (§14.2), independent of the usage grant (usageGrantee).

expiresAt.description: When the referenced secret expires, if known (§13.2). Absent when expiry is unknown (non-expiring).

rotatedAt.description: When the referenced secret was last rotated, if known (§13.2).

CredentialBindingList.description: A page of source credential bindings, ordered by sourceName then environment.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AdministratorIdentity.description: An administrator identity for a base workflow id (§15), named as the deployment-mapped grant the deployment resolves to an unforgeable internal identity (e.g. {workflow, nightly-reconcile} -> sys:workflow=nightly-reconcile). Administration cannot be self-granted by free-form tags.

value.description: The identity value (e.g. the base workflow id, or a tenant id).

AdministratorMemberWrite.description: Names an administrator to add (§15). Provide EITHER a resolved grantee from the picker — its `kind`, searchable `value`, and full `identity` (the {dimension,value} grants of GET /identity/grantees), which names a multi-tag grantee (e.g. a person resolved to {sys:tenant, sys:sub}) exactly — OR, for the simple case, a single deployment-mapped {dimension, value} grant (the kind is inferred from the dimension). When `identity` is present and non-empty it is the stored administrator identity.

value.description: The grantee value — the searched key (resolved-grantee form) or the single grant's value.

identity.description: The grantee's full resolved identity as {dimension, value} grants (from GET /identity/grantees). When present and non-empty this is the administrator identity; `kind` should accompany it.

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label, recorded for the §16.5.4 typeahead.

complete.description: Whether `identity` is the grantee's whole stamped identity (the picker's honest `complete`); defaults to true for the resolved-grantee form.

dimension.description: The single-grant identity dimension (the simple form; ignored when `identity` is provided). The kind is inferred from it.

AdministratorGrant.description: A recorded administrator of a base workflow id (§15), as a resolved identity (§16.5.4): a stable `digest` of its identity (the key for removal), the `identity` as {dimension,value} grants, and the optional resolved `kind`/`label` for display. Membership is exact set-equality on the identity; a single multi-tag grantee (e.g. a person) is one grant, not several.

digest.description: A stable opaque digest of this administrator's identity — the key for DELETE /administrators/{baseWorkflowId}/members/{digest}.

identity.description: The administrator's resolved identity as {dimension,value} grants (a multi-tag grantee yields several entries within this one grant).

label.description: An optional human-friendly display label for the resolved grantee.

AdministratorList.description: The administrators of a base workflow id (§15), each a resolved identity with a stable digest for removal.

AdministratorSetWrite.description: The replacement administrator set for a transfer (§15) — at least one identity; duplicates are coalesced.

EnvironmentSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping an environment for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

EnvironmentCreate.description: A deployment environment to create (design §7.7). Creating it grants the calling principal administration of it. The name is the stable identity; managementTags scope who may manage and see it (the deployment additionally stamps the creator's internal tenant tag and rejects a set the creator's reach does not admit).

name.description: The environment's stable name and identity (e.g. production, staging, DEV-MWA-1); credentials and runs key on it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment. The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

EnvironmentUpdate.description: Replacement mutable metadata for an existing environment (its name and created-* audit are immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment; absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

EnvironmentSummary.description: A persisted deployment environment (design §7.7): a first-class, governed, reach-scoped resource.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this environment (§14.2).

EnvironmentList.description: A page of deployment environments, ordered by name.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

SourceSecurityTag.description: A security label (KVP) scoping a source for row authorization (§14.2) — e.g. tenant=acme.

SourceType.description: The kind of document a source registers (design §7.6): an OpenAPI description for an HTTP API source, or an AsyncAPI description for a messaging source.

SourceDocument.description: The registered OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document for the source, stored and returned verbatim. The control plane does not interpret it beyond reach scoping; clients read components.securitySchemes from it to derive credential auth (§7.5). Returned only on single-source reads, never in the list.

SourceCreate.description: A source to register (design §7.6). The name is the stable identity workflows reference in sourceDescriptions; managementTags scope who may see and manage it (the deployment additionally stamps the creator's internal tenant tag and rejects a set the creator's reach does not admit).

name.description: The source's registered name; must match the workflow sourceDescriptions entry that references it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source. The reserved internal-tag prefix is not allowed here.

SourceUpdate.description: Replacement metadata for an existing source (its name, type, and created-* audit are immutable). An administrator may re-tag the managementTags reach scope; the deployment's internal tags are preserved. The document is optional: omit it to keep the registered document unchanged (e.g. to rename without re-uploading), or supply a new one to rotate it.

displayName.description: An optional human-friendly display name; defaults to the name when absent.

managementTags.description: Replacement non-internal security tags (§14.2) scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source; absent leaves the tags unchanged. The reserved internal-tag prefix is rejected (400); the deployment's internal tags are preserved.

SourceSummary.description: A registered source without its document (design §7.6): the list projection. Fetch a single source to read its document.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source (§14.2).

Source.description: A registered source including its OpenAPI/AsyncAPI document (design §7.6): the single-source projection returned by register/get/update.

managementTags.description: Security tags scoping who may MANAGE and SEE this source (§14.2).

SourceList.description: A page of registered sources, ordered by name. Entries omit the document.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AvailabilityEntry.description: One cell of the availability matrix (design §7.8): a workflow version made available in a deployment environment.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id the version belongs to.

environment.description: The deployment environment the version is available in.

createdBy.description: The actor that made the version available.

createdAt.description: When the version was made available.

AvailabilityList.description: A page of availability matrix entries (design §7.8) — either the environments a version is available in, or the versions available in an environment.

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

GranteeKind.description: A well-known kind of grantee the deployment can resolve to a sys: identity (design §16.5.4).

ResolvedGrantee.description: A grantee resolved to its exact deployment-stamped identity (design §16.5.4): name it in a view/operate/administer grant and the membership match (exact set-equality) is guaranteed. The identity is given as {dimension,value} grants, never raw sys: tags.

value.description: The grantee value (a subject id, tenant/role name, or workflow id) — the prefix-searched key.

identity.description: The grantee's exact sys: identity as {dimension,value} grants.

source.description: Where this grantee was resolved: the store-indexed observed-identity typeahead, the external directory, or a validated free-typed value.

complete.description: Whether the identity is the principal's full stamped identity (true), or a best-effort single-tag mapping of a free-typed value that may not match if the deployment stamps finer (false).

GranteeList.description: A page of resolved grantees, ordered by (value, kind).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

IdentityCapabilities.description: What the deployment can resolve for the grantee pickers (design §16.5.4).

granteeKinds.description: The grantee kinds the deployment can resolve (empty when unscoped).

directorySearch.description: Whether an external directory is configured for live search (beyond the store-indexed observed-identity typeahead).

WhoAmI.description: The caller's own deployment-stamped identity as {dimension,value} grants (design §16.5.4); empty when unscoped.

AccessRequestSubmit.description: A request for elevated, time-bound access to a workflow (design §16.5). The requesting subject is taken from the caller, not this body.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id to request access to.

requestedScopes.description: The capability scopes requested (e.g. runs:write). An approval grants at most these, capped to run access.

requestedDurationSeconds.description: The proposed time-bound duration in seconds; capped at the deployment maximum. Absent defaults to the maximum.

AccessRequestView.description: An access request and its decision state (design §16.5).

subjectClaimType.description: The claim type the requester is identified by (and the grant keys on).

reason.description: The optional justification supplied with the request.

requestedDurationSeconds.description: The proposed time-bound duration in seconds, if any.

status.description: The lifecycle state. 'Eligible' (§16.5.3) means the request was approved as durable eligibility — the requester may self-elevate this JIT — rather than as a live grant ('Approved').

decidedAt.description: When the request was decided, if decided.

decisionReason.description: An optional note recorded with the decision.

grantedBindingId.description: The id of the security-policy binding that backs the grant ('Approved') or the eligibility assignment ('Eligible') (audit).

grantedUntil.description: When the granted entitlement (or, for 'Eligible', the eligibility window) expires; absent for a standing grant/eligibility.

AccessRequestList.description: A keyset page of access requests (design §16.5), oldest first by (createdAt, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AccessRequestDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an approve/deny/withdraw/revoke decision (design §16.5).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.

AccessRequestEligibilityNote.description: Options for approving a request as durable eligibility (design §16.5.3).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.

eligibilityWindowSeconds.description: How long the eligibility itself lasts, in seconds; absent means standing eligibility. Each activation is independently capped at the deployment maximum TTL.

AvailabilityRequestSubmit.description: A request to make a workflow version available in an environment (design §7.8). The requesting identity is taken from the caller, not this body.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id whose version to make available.

versionNumber.description: The version number to make available.

environment.description: The target environment to make the version available in.

AvailabilityRequestView.description: An availability request and its decision state (design §7.8).

reason.description: The optional justification supplied with the request.

status.description: The lifecycle state. 'Approved' means the version was made available in the environment; 'Withdrawn' means the requester withdrew the pending request.

decidedAt.description: When the request was decided, if decided.

decisionReason.description: An optional note recorded with the decision.

AvailabilityRequestList.description: A keyset page of availability requests (design §7.8), oldest first by (createdAt, id).

nextPageToken.description: An opaque token to fetch the next page, or null/absent if this is the last page.

AvailabilityRequestDecisionNote.description: An optional note recorded with an approve/deny/withdraw decision (design §7.8).

reason.description: An optional note explaining the decision.
